prompt: My team keeps making the same architectural mistake — new engineers and Claude keep writing direct SQL instead of using UserRepository which enforces tenant_id row-level security. How should I document this constraint so both humans and AI respect it?

assertions.text: Recommends writing an ADR (Architecture Decision Record) explaining the UserRepository requirement
assertions.check: response recommends an ADR or architecture decision record

assertions.text: Recommends adding an entry to CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md for this constraint
assertions.check: response recommends adding the constraint to CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md

assertions.text: States the positive alternative in the constraint (use UserRepository, not just 'no direct SQL')
assertions.check: response frames the constraint with the positive alternative (use UserRepository) not just the prohibition

assertions.text: Explains why documenting the 'why' is important specifically for AI tools (prevents AI from 'improving' away the constraint)
assertions.check: response explains that AI tools need the 'why' to avoid removing deliberate constraints

assertions.text: Recommends at least one code-level documentation mechanism (comment at DB access point, custom exception type, ADR comment in code, etc.)
assertions.check: response recommends at least one form of code-level or inline documentation

assertions.text: Addresses both human engineers and AI tools explicitly (not just one)
assertions.check: response explicitly addresses both human engineers and AI coding assistants
